items.en: Leave no question unanswered.
items.note: No penalty for guessing

activities.title: Simulation compréhension
activities.intro: Mock questions mirroring the DELF B2 comprehension tasks.

questions.prompt: How long does the DELF B2 compréhension des écrits last?
questions.explanation: The reading section is allotted one hour on the DELF B2.

questions.prompt: In a mock listening, you miss a detail on the first play. What is best?
options: Note the gap and catch it on the second play
Rewind mentally and miss the next part
questions.answer: Note the gap and catch it on the second play
questions.explanation: You typically get two listens; mark the gap and target it the second time.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb meaning 'to justify' your answer (infinitive).
questions.explanation: 'Justifier' sa réponse means to support your answer with evidence from the text.

questions.prompt: Type the French noun for 'the instructions/guidelines' (plural) on an exam.
questions.explanation: 'Les consignes' are the task instructions you must read carefully.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Justify your answer by quoting the text.'
questions.explanation: 'En citant' (gerund) expresses the means: by quoting the text.

activities.title: Débriefing de l'examinateur
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) reviewing your mock comprehension with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment avez-vous géré votre temps sur la compréhension écrite ?
lines.en: How did you manage your time on the reading comprehension?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai consacré vingt minutes à chaque texte, puis dix minutes à la relecture.
lines.en: I spent twenty minutes on each text, then ten minutes reviewing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quelle a été la question la plus difficile à l'oral ?
lines.en: Which was the hardest question in the listening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une question sur l'implicite : le ton ironique m'avait échappé.
lines.en: A question on implied meaning: the ironic tone had escaped me.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comment comptez-vous corriger cela ?
lines.en: How do you plan to correct that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En écoutant davantage d'émissions où le ton et l'ironie sont marqués.
lines.en: By listening to more programmes where tone and irony are marked.

days.title: Examen blanc DELF B2, production (écrite et orale)
days.objective: Complete a timed mock of the written and oral production sections.
days.paretoFocus: Respect the 250-word target and the oral prep-then-present timing.
drills: Write a 250-word argumentative essay in 60 minutes
Prepare a document in 30 minutes then present it in 20
Self-assess your essay against the DELF B2 criteria grid

items: Recall the word count expected for the production écrite
Correct three grammar errors in your mock essay
Re-record a 90-second improved oral introduction

activities.title: Critères d'évaluation de la production
activities.intro: What the examiner grades in written and oral production.

items.hr: La production écrite compte environ 250 mots.
items.en: The written production runs to about 250 words.

items.hr: L'examinateur évalue la cohérence et la richesse lexicale.
items.en: The examiner assesses coherence and lexical range.

items.hr: Respectez le registre formel exigé par la consigne.
items.en: Respect the formal register required by the instructions.

items.hr: Vous disposez de 30 minutes de préparation pour l'oral.
items.en: You have 30 minutes of preparation for the oral.

items.hr: Variez vos connecteurs pour éviter les répétitions.
items.en: Vary your connectors to avoid repetition.

activities.title: Simulation production
activities.intro: Mock questions about the production tasks and criteria.

questions.prompt: Approximately how many words should the DELF B2 essay contain?
questions.explanation: The production écrite targets around 250 words.

questions.prompt: How much preparation time do you get before the oral presentation?
questions.explanation: You prepare your document and monologue for 30 minutes before presenting.

questions.prompt: Type the French noun for 'coherence' assessed in your writing.
questions.explanation: 'La cohérence' (coherence) is a key marking criterion.

questions.prompt: Type the French phrase 'the formal register' (le registre ...).
questions.explanation: 'Le registre formel' is expected in the DELF B2 essay and debate.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Vary your vocabulary to enrich your text.'
questions.explanation: 'Pour enrichir' expresses purpose: in order to enrich your text.

activities.title: Passation de l'oral blanc
activities.intro: Play 'Me' (the candidate) sitting the mock oral with the examiner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vous avez eu trente minutes de préparation. Vous pouvez commencer.
lines.en: You had thirty minutes of preparation. You may begin.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Merci. Le document que je vais présenter porte sur la consommation responsable.
lines.en: Thank you. The document I will present is about responsible consumption.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quelle position défendez-vous ?
lines.en: What position do you defend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je défends l'idée que de petits gestes quotidiens ont un réel impact.
lines.en: I defend the idea that small daily actions have a real impact.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certains jugent ces gestes dérisoires. Que leur répondez-vous ?
lines.en: Some consider these actions trivial. What do you answer them?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je réponds que, cumulés à grande échelle, ces gestes deviennent décisifs.
lines.en: I answer that, added up on a large scale, these actions become decisive.

days.title: Le passé simple : le reconnaître à l'écrit
days.objective: You can recognise passé simple forms in novels, history books and press features, and match them to their infinitives without confusing them with other tenses.
days.paretoFocus: Almost every French novel, biography and museum caption narrates in the passé simple, so reading comfort collapses if you cannot decode it, even though you will never need to speak it.
drills: Take any French story text and underline every verb that ends in -a, -it, -irent or -èrent, then write its infinitive next to it.
Rewrite five passé simple sentences in the passé composé and say the spoken version aloud.
List the six irregular stems you meet most often (etre, avoir, faire, venir, prendre, voir) and drill them from infinitive to third person.

items: Which endings mark the passé simple of -er verbs in the third person singular and plural?
How do you tell 'il eut' from 'il eût' when reading?
Which spoken tense replaces the passé simple in conversation?

activities.title: Reconnaître le passé simple
activities.intro: The passé simple is the narrative past of written French: it reports finished events, one after another. You only need to read it, never produce it in speech, where the passé composé takes over. Learn the third person forms first, since narration is almost entirely third person.

items.hr: Il ouvrit la porte et sortit sans un mot.
items.en: He opened the door and went out without a word.
items.note: ouvrir -> il ouvrit ; sortir -> il sortit

items.hr: Elle prit son manteau et descendit l'escalier.
items.en: She took her coat and went down the stairs.
items.note: prendre -> elle prit ; descendre -> elle descendit

items.hr: Les voyageurs arrivèrent très tard dans la nuit.
items.en: The travellers arrived very late at night.
items.note: -er verbs: ils arrivèrent

items.hr: Nous fûmes surpris par la brutalité de sa réponse.
items.en: We were surprised by the harshness of his reply.
items.note: être -> nous fûmes

items.hr: Il eut soudain envie de tout abandonner.
items.en: He suddenly felt like giving everything up.
items.note: avoir -> il eut, with no accent

activities.title: Décoder la narration écrite
activities.intro: Practise matching passé simple forms to their infinitives and telling them apart from other past tenses.

questions.prompt: In 'Il vint nous voir le lendemain', which infinitive does 'vint' come from?
questions.explanation: Venir has the irregular passé simple stem vin-: je vins, il vint, ils vinrent.

questions.prompt: Which of these sentences is written in the passé simple?
options: Elle a répondu tout de suite.
Elle va répondre tout de suite.
questions.explanation: The ending -it on a -re verb marks the passé simple; -ait would be the imparfait and 'a répondu' the passé composé.

questions.prompt: Rewrite in the narrative past tense of literature: 'Il a compris son erreur.' Il ___ son erreur.
questions.explanation: Comprendre follows prendre: il prit, il comprit.

questions.prompt: Third person plural, narrative past of the verb 'faire': Les ouvriers ___ le nécessaire.
questions.explanation: Faire has the stem fi-: il fit, ils firent.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The king died the following winter.'
questions.explanation: Mourir gives il mourut in the passé simple, the standard tense of historical narration.

activities.title: Devant un roman du dix-neuvième siècle
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend reads a page of a classic novel aloud and you help her identify the passé simple forms.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Écoute : « Il traversa la cour, frappa deux fois, puis attendit. » Ce sont des passés composés ?
lines.en: Listen: 'He crossed the courtyard, knocked twice, then waited.' Are those passé composé forms?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, c'est du passé simple. Traversa, frappa et attendit sont des verbes d'action au récit écrit.
lines.en: No, that is the passé simple. Traversa, frappa and attendit are action verbs in written narration.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et « ils furent » dans la phrase suivante, c'est quel verbe ?
lines.en: And 'ils furent' in the next sentence, which verb is that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est le verbe être. Au passé simple, on a je fus, il fut, ils furent.
lines.en: That is the verb être. In the passé simple you get je fus, il fut, ils furent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc si je racontais la même scène à voix haute, je dirais quoi ?
lines.en: So if I told the same scene out loud, what would I say?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tu dirais : il a traversé la cour, il a frappé deux fois, puis il a attendu.
lines.en: You would say: he crossed the courtyard, he knocked twice, then he waited.

days.title: Le subjonctif imparfait : le reconnaître dans un texte littéraire
days.objective: You can identify imperfect subjunctive forms in literary texts, understand why they appear after a past main verb, and restate the sentence in modern French.
days.paretoFocus: This tense is dead in speech but alive in classic literature and in a few fixed phrases, so recognising it stops you from misreading a whole sentence as a passé simple.
drills: Find three sentences with 'qu'il fût', 'qu'il eût' or 'qu'il vînt' and rewrite each with the present subjunctive.
Write down the pairs that differ only by an accent: il eut / qu'il eût, il fut / qu'il fût.
Say aloud the modern equivalent of five literary sentences you have decoded.

items: What triggers the imperfect subjunctive in a literary sentence?
Which accent distinguishes the third person imperfect subjunctive from the passé simple?
Which tense stands in for it in today's French?

activities.title: Le subjonctif imparfait en littérature
activities.intro: In older written French, when the main verb was in a past tense, any following subjunctive shifted to its imperfect form. Modern French simply uses the present subjunctive instead. The third person singular always carries a circumflex, which is your main reading clue.

items.hr: Il fallait qu'il partît avant l'aube.
items.en: He had to leave before dawn.
items.note: partir -> qu'il partît ; modern: qu'il parte

items.hr: Elle craignait que son frère ne fût déjà loin.
items.en: She feared that her brother was already far away.
items.note: être -> qu'il fût ; modern: qu'il soit

items.hr: Nous voulions qu'elle eût le temps de répondre.
items.en: We wanted her to have time to answer.
items.note: avoir -> qu'elle eût ; modern: qu'elle ait

items.hr: Le maître ordonna qu'on ouvrît toutes les fenêtres.
items.en: The master ordered that all the windows be opened.
items.note: ouvrir -> qu'on ouvrît

items.hr: Personne ne pensait qu'il vînt si tôt.
items.en: Nobody thought he would come so early.
items.note: venir -> qu'il vînt ; compare il vint, passé simple

activities.title: Lire une page classique
activities.intro: Practise telling this literary tense apart from the passé simple and converting it to modern usage.

questions.prompt: In 'Il aurait fallu qu'elle vînt', what is 'vînt'?
questions.explanation: The subordinate clause after 'il aurait fallu que' demands a subjunctive, and the circumflex marks the imperfect subjunctive rather than the passé simple 'vint'.

questions.prompt: Which form is the imperfect subjunctive of 'avoir' in the third person singular?
questions.explanation: 'Qu'il ait' is the present subjunctive and 'il eut' without accent is the passé simple; the circumflex is the whole difference.

questions.prompt: Modernise this literary sentence with the present subjunctive: 'Il fallait qu'il fît attention.' Il fallait qu'il ___ attention.
questions.explanation: Modern French keeps the present subjunctive even after a past main verb, so fît becomes fasse.

questions.prompt: Complete this literary sentence with the imperfect subjunctive of the verb 'être': Il était rare qu'elle ___ en retard.
questions.explanation: The imperfect subjunctive of être in the third person singular is fût, with a circumflex that separates it from the passé simple 'fut'.

questions.prompt: Build the literary French for: 'She wanted him to leave at once.'
questions.explanation: A past main verb plus a subjunctive clause gives the imperfect subjunctive partît in literary style.

activities.title: Un style d'un autre siècle
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your reading partner is puzzled by circumflexes in an old novel and you explain the form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: « Il souhaitait qu'elle revînt avant la nuit. » Pourquoi cet accent sur le i ?
lines.en: 'He wished she would come back before nightfall.' Why that accent on the i?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parce que c'est un subjonctif imparfait. Sans accent, revint serait un passé simple.
lines.en: Because it is an imperfect subjunctive. Without the accent, revint would be a passé simple.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et aujourd'hui, on écrirait comment ?
lines.en: And today, how would we write it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: On écrirait : il souhaitait qu'elle revienne avant la nuit. Le présent du subjonctif suffit.
lines.en: We would write: he wished she would come back before nightfall. The present subjunctive is enough.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Donc je n'aurai jamais à l'employer moi-même ?
lines.en: So I will never have to use it myself?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jamais à l'oral. Il faut seulement le reconnaître quand tu lis des textes anciens.
lines.en: Never in speech. You only need to recognise it when you read older texts.

days.title: La modalisation : nuancer un propos
days.objective: You can soften or qualify a statement in French using hedging adverbs, the conditional of unverified information and impersonal expressions of probability.
days.paretoFocus: Educated French rarely asserts flatly: hedging is what separates a credible opinion from a naive one in essays, meetings and news reporting.
drills: Take five flat statements you believe and rewrite each with a different hedge.
Read a news item and mark every conditional that reports unconfirmed information.
Rank five hedges from weakest to strongest commitment and say them aloud in a sentence.

items: How does French mark information it cannot confirm?
Which mood follows 'il semblerait que' and 'il n'est pas exclu que'?
Give two adverbs that soften a claim without denying it.

activities.title: Nuancer ce que l'on affirme
activities.intro: Modalisation is the set of tools that shows how strongly you commit to what you say. French uses adverbs, impersonal verbs and, above all, the conditional to report information it has not verified. Notice which of these expressions demand the subjunctive.

items.hr: Il semblerait que la réunion ait été reportée.
items.en: It would seem that the meeting has been postponed.
items.note: il semblerait que + subjonctif

items.hr: Le nombre de participants serait plus élevé que prévu.
items.en: The number of participants is reportedly higher than expected.
items.note: conditional = information not confirmed

items.hr: Cette solution est sans doute la moins coûteuse.
items.en: This solution is probably the least expensive one.
items.note: sans doute means probably, not 'without any doubt'

items.hr: On peut raisonnablement penser que la tendance va durer.
items.en: One can reasonably think that the trend will last.
items.note: cautious way to give an opinion in writing

items.hr: Il n'est pas exclu qu'un nouveau report soit annoncé.
items.en: A further postponement cannot be ruled out.
items.note: il n'est pas exclu que + subjonctif

activities.title: Peser ses mots
activities.intro: Practise turning blunt assertions into carefully qualified statements.

questions.prompt: Which sentence presents the information as unverified?
options: Le directeur aurait démissionné ce matin.
Le directeur a démissionné ce matin.
Le directeur va démissionner ce matin.
questions.answer: Le directeur aurait démissionné ce matin.
questions.explanation: The past conditional used journalistically signals that the speaker reports a claim without confirming it.

questions.prompt: 'Il semblerait que la situation ___ se dégrader.' Which completion is correct?
questions.explanation: Il semblerait que expresses uncertainty and takes the subjunctive, so pouvoir becomes puisse.

questions.prompt: Insert the two-word adverbial meaning 'probably': Cette hypothèse est ___ la plus solide.
questions.explanation: Sans doute in modern French means probably; only sans aucun doute expresses certainty.

questions.prompt: Turn the assertion into reported, unconfirmed information: 'Les négociations ont échoué.' Les négociations ___ échoué.
questions.explanation: The auxiliary goes into the conditional to mark information attributed to a source rather than asserted.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It would seem that the figures have been revised.'
questions.explanation: Il semblerait que governs the past subjunctive aient été révisés.

activities.title: Une information à vérifier
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague brings you a rumour and you insist on wording it cautiously.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: L'usine ferme en décembre, c'est sûr, tout le monde en parle.
lines.en: The factory is closing in December, it is certain, everyone is talking about it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tant que rien n'est officiel, écrivons plutôt : l'usine fermerait en décembre.
lines.en: As long as nothing is official, let us rather write: the factory would reportedly close in December.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour les trois cents emplois menacés ?
lines.en: And what about the three hundred jobs at risk?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il semblerait que trois cents postes soient concernés. On garde le conditionnel et le subjonctif.
lines.en: It would seem that three hundred posts are affected. We keep the conditional and the subjunctive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu crois vraiment qu'il faut être aussi prudent ?
lines.en: Do you really think we need to be that cautious?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui. Il n'est pas exclu que la direction démente tout demain matin.
lines.en: Yes. It cannot be ruled out that management will deny everything tomorrow morning.

days.title: Les connecteurs d'articulation complexes : or, néanmoins, en revanche
days.objective: You can use or, néanmoins and en revanche accurately to overturn, concede and contrast, instead of relying on mais and pourtant.
days.paretoFocus: These three connectors carry the logical skeleton of formal French writing and speaking, and using them correctly instantly raises the register of an argument.
drills: Write one short argument that uses or, then néanmoins, then en revanche in that order.
Take five sentences built with mais and replace mais with a more precise connector.
Read a news commentary and note which connector opens each new step of the reasoning.

items: Which connector introduces a fact that overturns the expectation just created?
What is the difference between néanmoins and en revanche?
Where does the comma go after each of these connectors?

activities.title: Articuler un raisonnement
activities.intro: Or introduces a new fact that changes everything and prepares a conclusion. Néanmoins concedes: what precedes stays true, but it does not stop the second idea. En revanche balances two facts of opposite sign, one positive and one negative.

items.hr: Le projet paraissait simple ; or, il a coûté trois fois plus cher.
items.en: The project looked simple; and yet it cost three times more.
items.note: or brings in the decisive fact

items.hr: Le texte est bien écrit ; néanmoins, il reste beaucoup trop long.
items.en: The text is well written; nevertheless, it is still far too long.
items.note: néanmoins concedes the first point

items.hr: Les ventes baissent en France ; en revanche, elles progressent à l'étranger.
items.en: Sales are falling in France; on the other hand, they are rising abroad.
items.note: en revanche balances two symmetrical facts

items.hr: Tout le monde promet des réformes ; or personne ne les finance.
items.en: Everyone promises reforms; and yet nobody funds them.
items.note: or can be used without a following comma

items.hr: Nous acceptons le principe ; néanmoins, le calendrier nous inquiète.
items.en: We accept the principle; nevertheless, the timetable worries us.
items.note: typical formula in a negotiation

activities.title: Choisir le bon connecteur
activities.intro: Practise picking the connector whose logic matches the relation between two ideas.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a fact that overturns what has just been said and prepares a conclusion?
questions.explanation: Or is the connector of the second premise: it brings the fact that makes the conclusion inevitable.

questions.prompt: 'Le premier candidat manque d'expérience ; ___, il est très motivé.' Which connector expresses a balanced contrast between two aspects?
questions.explanation: En revanche opposes a drawback and an advantage of the same subject, without conceding anything.

questions.prompt: Insert the formal one-word equivalent of 'pourtant', which is neither cependant nor toutefois: Le rapport est incomplet ; ___, il mérite d'être lu.
questions.explanation: Néanmoins concedes the criticism while keeping the second statement valid.

questions.prompt: Insert the connector that signals the decisive new fact: Tous annonçaient une victoire facile ; ___, le match a été perdu.
questions.explanation: Or introduces the fact that contradicts the expectation built by the first clause.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The method is expensive; nevertheless, it works.'
questions.explanation: Néanmoins opens the second clause and concedes the cost without cancelling the result.

activities.title: Débat sur le télétravail
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague argues against remote work and you answer using precise connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Le télétravail isole les équipes, donc il faut revenir au bureau cinq jours par semaine.
lines.en: Remote work isolates teams, so we should go back to the office five days a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'isolement existe, néanmoins il se corrige avec deux journées communes par semaine.
lines.en: The isolation is real; nevertheless, it can be fixed with two shared days a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La productivité baisse aussi, tous les managers le disent.
lines.en: Productivity is also falling, all the managers say so.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Or les chiffres du service montrent l'inverse depuis deux ans.
lines.en: And yet the department's figures have shown the opposite for two years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la formation des nouveaux, à distance, c'est compliqué.
lines.en: And training newcomers remotely is complicated.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sur ce point je te suis. En revanche, les réunions de suivi fonctionnent mieux à distance.
lines.en: On that point I agree with you. On the other hand, follow-up meetings work better remotely.

days.title: La nominalisation avancée dans la presse
days.objective: You can turn a full sentence into the noun phrase used in headlines and summaries, choosing the right derived noun for a given verb.
days.paretoFocus: Headlines, report titles and executive summaries are built almost entirely of nominalisations, so this one transformation unlocks a huge amount of written French.
drills: Turn ten sentences from a news page into headline noun phrases.
Build a list of verb and noun pairs you keep meeting, such as augmenter and la hausse.
Write a five-line news summary in which every line starts with a noun.

items: Which preposition links the nominalised action to its agent?
Give the noun for interrompre, évacuer and refuser.
Why do headlines drop the article and the verb?

activities.title: Des titres sans verbe
activities.intro: Press headlines compress a whole clause into a noun phrase: the verb becomes a noun and the subject follows with de. The article is usually dropped at the start of the headline. Learning the derived noun for each common verb is the real work here.

items.hr: Hausse des prix à la consommation en juin.
items.en: Rise in consumer prices in June.
items.note: augmenter -> la hausse

items.hr: Report de la réunion au mois prochain.
items.en: Postponement of the meeting until next month.
items.note: reporter -> le report

items.hr: Refus du gouvernement de rouvrir les négociations.
items.en: The government's refusal to reopen negotiations.
items.note: refuser -> le refus ; agent introduced by de

items.hr: Interruption du trafic sur trois lignes de métro.
items.en: Traffic interruption on three underground lines.
items.note: interrompre -> l'interruption

items.hr: Évacuation des habitants du quartier nord.
items.en: Evacuation of the residents of the northern district.
items.note: évacuer -> l'évacuation

activities.title: Écrire des titres
activities.intro: Practise deriving the correct noun and building headline noun phrases.

questions.prompt: Which noun corresponds to the verb 'accroître'?
questions.explanation: Verbs in -oître often take the suffix -issement, as in accroissement.

questions.prompt: Which headline correctly nominalises 'Le président a démissionné'?
questions.explanation: The noun of démissionner is démission, and the subject becomes a complement with du.

questions.prompt: Write the headline noun matching 'Un train a déraillé' : ___ d'un train en Normandie.
questions.explanation: Dérailler gives déraillement, one of the many -ment nouns built on -er verbs.

questions.prompt: Give the headline noun built on the verb 'baisser': ___ du chômage au deuxième trimestre.
questions.explanation: Baisser gives the short noun baisse, the standard opposite of hausse in economic headlines.

questions.prompt: Build the French headline for: 'Closure of three factories announced.'
questions.explanation: Fermer gives fermeture, and the past participle annoncée agrees with the feminine head noun.

activities.title: À la rédaction
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An editor asks you to compress full sentences into headlines using nominalisation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ton titre fait douze mots : « La mairie a décidé de fermer la piscine municipale ».
lines.en: Your headline is twelve words long: 'The town hall has decided to close the municipal swimming pool.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je nominalise : Fermeture de la piscine municipale, décision de la mairie.
lines.en: I will nominalise it: Closure of the municipal swimming pool, a town hall decision.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mieux. Et pour « Les usagers ont protesté toute la semaine » ?
lines.en: Better. And for 'Users protested all week'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Protestation des usagers pendant toute la semaine.
lines.en: Protest by users throughout the week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si les travaux commencent en septembre ?
lines.en: And if the works start in September?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ouverture du chantier en septembre. Un nom, un complément, pas de verbe.
lines.en: Start of works in September. One noun, one complement, no verb.

days.title: Les registres de langue : soutenu, courant, familier
days.objective: You can recognise the three main registers of French and say the same thing in the register that fits your listener and situation.
days.paretoFocus: Register mistakes are the fastest way to sound odd in French: the words are correct, the situation is not, and native speakers notice immediately.
drills: Take one request and write it three times: soutenu, courant, familier.
Collect five familiar words you hear and write their neutral equivalents.
Practise a polite formula out loud until it feels automatic before a real phone call.

items: Which register drops the ne of negation and why?
Give a soutenu equivalent of 'demander'.
Which register would you use in a written complaint to a company?

activities.title: Trois façons de dire la même chose
activities.intro: French has three practical registers. Soutenu uses long formulas, inversion and rare verbs, and appears in formal writing. Courant is the safe default. Familier drops the ne, shortens words and uses slangier nouns, and belongs among friends only.

items.hr: Auriez-vous l'obligeance de patienter quelques instants ?
items.en: Would you be so kind as to wait a few moments?
items.note: soutenu

items.hr: Pouvez-vous patienter un instant, s'il vous plaît ?
items.en: Can you wait a moment, please?
items.note: courant

items.hr: Tu peux attendre deux minutes ?
items.en: Can you wait two minutes?
items.note: familier, tutoiement and no formula

items.hr: Je vous prie de bien vouloir m'excuser de ce retard.
items.en: Please accept my apologies for this delay.
items.note: soutenu, standard in a formal message

items.hr: J'ai un truc à finir, je file.
items.en: I have got something to finish, I am off.
items.note: familier: truc, filer

activities.title: Ajuster le registre
activities.intro: Practise identifying the register of a sentence and converting it to another one.

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs to the register soutenu?
options: Je suis navré de ne pouvoir vous répondre.
Désolé, je peux pas vous répondre.
Je peux pas te répondre, là.
J'ai pas de réponse, tant pis.
questions.answer: Je suis navré de ne pouvoir vous répondre.
questions.explanation: Navré, the full negation and the infinitive without de after pouvoir are all marks of formal French.

questions.prompt: 'Ce boulot me gonfle.' belongs to which register?
questions.explanation: Boulot and gonfler in this sense are informal and would be out of place at work with a client.

questions.prompt: Rewrite in a neutral register: 'Il est vachement content.' Il est ___ content.
questions.explanation: Vachement is a familiar intensifier; the neutral equivalent is très.

questions.prompt: Give the formal verb that replaces 'demander' in a written request: Je me permets de ___ votre indulgence.
questions.explanation: Solliciter is the formal verb for asking for something in official correspondence.

questions.prompt: Build the formal French for: 'Would you be so kind as to confirm your attendance?'
questions.explanation: Avoir l'amabilité de plus infinitive is a standard soutenu request formula, with inversion instead of est-ce que.

activities.title: Du bureau au café
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You answer the same question twice, first to a client on the phone, then to a friend, switching register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour, pourriez-vous me confirmer la date de livraison ?
lines.en: Hello, could you confirm the delivery date for me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bien entendu. Je vous confirme la livraison pour le douze, et je vous prie d'excuser ce délai.
lines.en: Of course. I confirm delivery for the twelfth, and I apologise for the delay.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Je vous remercie de votre diligence.
lines.en: Thank you for your promptness.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vous en prie. Je reste à votre disposition pour toute question.
lines.en: You are welcome. I remain available for any question.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Alors, ce fameux colis, il arrive quand ?
lines.en: So, that famous parcel, when is it coming?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le douze, normalement. Franchement, j'y crois moyennement, mais bon.
lines.en: The twelfth, in theory. Honestly, I only half believe it, but never mind.

days.title: L'implicite et le sous-entendu
days.objective: You can detect what a French sentence implies without stating it, and produce hints yourself using presupposing words like encore, finir par and même.
days.paretoFocus: A large part of everyday French disagreement, complaint and politeness is never said outright, so missing implicature means missing the actual message.
drills: Write five sentences that carry a reproach without containing a single negative word.
Listen to a conversation and note one thing that was meant but not said.
Rewrite three blunt criticisms as implied ones, then compare the effect.

items: What does 'encore' presuppose about an earlier event?
What does 'il a fini par accepter' imply about the earlier attitude?
How can a simple question function as a reproach?

activities.title: Ce qui n'est pas dit
activities.intro: Some French words carry information that survives even if you negate the sentence: that is presupposition. Other sentences suggest a meaning through context alone, which is implicature. Both let a speaker criticise, complain or doubt without ever saying it directly.

items.hr: Il a encore oublié les clés.
items.en: He has forgotten the keys again.
items.note: encore presupposes it happened before

items.hr: Je ne dis pas que c'est faux.
items.en: I am not saying it is wrong.
items.note: suggests serious doubt without asserting it

items.hr: Elle a fini par accepter la proposition.
items.en: She ended up accepting the proposal.
items.note: finir par implies earlier resistance

items.hr: Même Antoine a compris le problème.
items.en: Even Antoine understood the problem.
items.note: même implies he was the least likely to

items.hr: Tu as vu l'heure ?
items.en: Have you seen the time?
items.note: not a question about time: a reproach

activities.title: Lire entre les lignes
activities.intro: Practise identifying the hidden meaning of a sentence and producing it yourself.

questions.prompt: 'Même Paul a réussi l'exercice.' What is implied about Paul?
options: Il était le moins susceptible d'y arriver.
Il a réussi avant les autres.
Il a expliqué l'exercice aux autres.
questions.answer: Il était le moins susceptible d'y arriver.
questions.explanation: Même places its element at the low end of an implicit scale of expectation.

questions.prompt: Which sentence presupposes that the person used to smoke?
questions.explanation: Arrêter de presupposes that the activity was going on before, and the presupposition survives negation.

questions.prompt: Add the adverb that implies the same thing had already happened before: Tu es ___ en retard !
questions.explanation: Encore turns a plain statement into a reproach by presupposing a repetition.

questions.prompt: Complete so that the speaker hints at doubt without asserting it: Je ne ___ pas que tu as tort, mais réfléchis encore.
questions.explanation: Je ne dis pas que is a classic hedge that signals disagreement while appearing to withhold it.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'She ended up admitting it.'
questions.explanation: Finir par plus infinitive implies that the outcome came after resistance.

activities.title: Entre colocataires
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your flatmate never does the washing up and you complain entirely by implication.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Salut ! Tu as passé une bonne journée ?
lines.en: Hi! Did you have a good day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Très bonne. J'ai encore retrouvé la vaisselle de ce matin dans l'évier, remarque.
lines.en: Very good. I found this morning's washing up still in the sink, mind you.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ah oui, j'allais la faire, justement.
lines.en: Oh yes, I was just about to do it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je ne dis pas que tu ne la fais jamais. Tu finis toujours par la faire.
lines.en: I am not saying you never do it. You always end up doing it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bon, j'ai compris le message.
lines.en: Right, I get the message.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quel message ? Je te demandais juste si tu avais vu l'heure.
lines.en: What message? I was only asking whether you had seen the time.

days.title: L'ironie et l'humour dans un texte
days.objective: You can spot French irony from its verbal signals and context mismatch, and produce simple ironic remarks yourself.
days.paretoFocus: French humour leans heavily on saying the opposite with a straight face, so taking an ironic sentence literally can turn a joke into a misunderstanding.
drills: Write three ironic compliments about a small everyday failure.
Find an ironic line in a French comment section and explain what is really meant.
Say the same sentence twice aloud, once sincerely and once ironically, and hear the difference.

items: Which two signals usually reveal irony in a written French sentence?
What does 'C'est malin !' actually mean when a mistake has just been made?
Why can exaggerated praise be a criticism?

activities.title: Dire le contraire de ce qu'on pense
activities.intro: French irony works by praising in a situation that clearly does not deserve praise. In writing, the clues are the mismatch with the facts, exaggerated positive words and exclamation marks. The tone is usually light, but the message underneath is a criticism.

items.hr: Bravo, tu as vraiment choisi le bon moment.
items.en: Well done, you really picked the right moment.
items.note: ironic praise, said after bad timing

items.hr: Il fait un temps magnifique, comme d'habitude.
items.en: The weather is magnificent, as usual.
items.note: said in pouring rain

items.hr: C'est malin ! Il va falloir tout recommencer.
items.en: That was clever! Now we will have to start again.
items.note: malin used ironically means the opposite

items.hr: Quelle organisation ! Trois heures de retard au départ.
items.en: What organisation! Three hours late at departure.
items.note: positive noun plus a damning fact

items.hr: Je ne suis pas en retard, c'est l'heure qui est en avance.
items.en: I am not late, it is the time that is early.
items.note: absurd humour used to defuse a reproach

activities.title: Repérer l'ironie
activities.intro: Practise deciding whether a line is meant literally and building ironic remarks of your own.

questions.prompt: Said in pouring rain, 'Quel temps splendide !' is...
questions.explanation: The praise contradicts the observable facts, which is the core mechanism of irony.

questions.prompt: Which reply to a meeting invitation is ironic?
options: La réunion commence à neuf heures.
questions.explanation: Quel bonheur is enthusiastic praise attached to something the speaker clearly dislikes.

questions.prompt: Complete the ironic exclamation used to mock a clumsy mistake: C'est ___ ! Tout est à refaire.
questions.explanation: Malin means clever, and used as an exclamation after a blunder it means exactly the opposite.

questions.prompt: Insert an adjective of praise that makes the remark ironic: Quelle idée ___ ! Nous avons perdu deux jours.
questions.explanation: The strongly positive adjective clashes with the stated loss, which produces the ironic reading.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Well done, you have broken everything.'
questions.explanation: Bravo opens the sentence as praise and the second clause reveals it as reproach.

activities.title: Un week-end parfaitement réussi
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend describes a disastrous trip and you answer with steady irony.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On a attendu le train trois heures sous la pluie, sans information.
lines.en: We waited three hours for the train in the rain, with no information.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quelle organisation exemplaire. Vous avez dû adorer.
lines.en: What exemplary organisation. You must have loved it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et l'hôtel avait perdu notre réservation.
lines.en: And the hotel had lost our booking.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bravo, un vrai service de luxe. C'était cher, au moins ?
lines.en: Well done, real luxury service. Was it expensive, at least?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deux cents euros la nuit, et le chauffage ne marchait pas.
lines.en: Two hundred euros a night, and the heating did not work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: C'est malin, ils t'ont vendu l'air frais en supplément. Tu y retournes quand ?
lines.en: That was clever, they sold you fresh air as an extra. When are you going back?

days.title: Les expressions idiomatiques et les proverbes
days.objective: You can understand and use a core set of French idioms and proverbs, and recognise that their meaning is not the sum of their words.
days.paretoFocus: Idioms appear constantly in speech, headlines and workplace talk, and a single unknown image can hide the meaning of an entire sentence.
drills: Pick three idioms and use each in a sentence about your own week.
Match five French proverbs with the closest saying in your own language and note where the image differs.
Keep a running list of idioms you hear, with the situation in which they were used.

items: What does 'prendre ses jambes à son cou' mean?
Which proverb warns against judging by appearances?
What does it mean to say that someone 'a le bras long'?

activities.title: Images et proverbes du quotidien
activities.intro: An idiom has a fixed form: you cannot change its words or its number without breaking it. A proverb is a short sentence that states a piece of collective wisdom and is quoted whole. Both are extremely frequent in ordinary French.

items.hr: En voyant les policiers, il a pris ses jambes à son cou.
items.en: When he saw the police, he took to his heels.
items.note: prendre ses jambes à son cou = run away fast

items.hr: Ce dossier est long, mais ce n'est pas la mer à boire.
items.en: This file is long, but it is not that hard.
items.note: ce n'est pas la mer à boire = it is not such a big deal

items.hr: Le client est intéressé : il faut battre le fer tant qu'il est chaud.
items.en: The client is interested: we must strike while the iron is hot.
items.note: proverb about acting at the right moment

items.hr: Ne t'inquiète pas : petit à petit, l'oiseau fait son nid.
items.en: Do not worry: little by little, the bird builds its nest.
items.note: proverb about slow, steady progress

items.hr: Demande-lui, elle a le bras long dans ce ministère.
items.en: Ask her, she has a lot of influence in that ministry.
items.note: avoir le bras long = be well connected

activities.title: Comprendre les images
activities.intro: Practise decoding common idioms and completing well known proverbs.

questions.prompt: 'Tomber dans les pommes' means...
questions.explanation: The idiom means to faint; the apples have nothing to do with the meaning, which is why it must be learned whole.

questions.prompt: Which proverb warns that appearances are misleading?
options: L'habit ne fait pas le moine.
Qui va à la chasse perd sa place.
Après la pluie, le beau temps.
Les bons comptes font les bons amis.
questions.answer: L'habit ne fait pas le moine.
questions.explanation: Literally the garment does not make the monk, that is, you cannot judge someone by their outward look.

questions.prompt: Complete the idiom meaning that a task is not so difficult: Ce n'est pas la ___ à boire.
questions.explanation: The image is drinking the sea, an impossible task, so the negation means the job is manageable.

questions.prompt: Complete the proverb about seizing the right moment: Il faut battre le ___ tant qu'il est chaud.
questions.explanation: The blacksmith image gives the proverb: act while conditions are favourable.

questions.prompt: Build the French idiom for: 'He took to his heels.'
questions.explanation: The idiom is fixed: the possessives ses and son cannot be changed.

activities.title: Un projet qui traîne
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague is discouraged by a slow project and you answer with idioms and proverbs.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On avance de trois pages par semaine. On n'y arrivera jamais.
lines.en: We are moving forward three pages a week. We will never get there.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Petit à petit, l'oiseau fait son nid. Le rapport sera prêt en juin.
lines.en: Little by little, the bird builds its nest. The report will be ready in June.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il reste tout le chapitre juridique à écrire.
lines.en: And the whole legal chapter is still to be written.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ce n'est pas la mer à boire. Demande à Claire, elle a le bras long au service juridique.
lines.en: It is not such a big deal. Ask Claire, she is well connected in the legal department.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Elle est disponible cette semaine seulement, je crois.
lines.en: She is only available this week, I think.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Alors écris-lui aujourd'hui : il faut battre le fer tant qu'il est chaud.
lines.en: Then write to her today: you must strike while the iron is hot.

days.title: La comparaison et la métaphore à l'écrit
days.objective: You can build comparisons with comme, tel and correlative structures, and tell a simile apart from a metaphor when reading or writing.
days.paretoFocus: Images make written argument vivid and memorable, and French essays and commentary are judged partly on whether your comparisons are precise rather than flat.
drills: Describe your city three times: once literally, once with a simile, once with a metaphor.
Find two images in a French article and decide whether each is a comparison or a metaphor.
Write three sentences using plus... plus... and moins... moins...

items: What is the formal difference between a comparison and a metaphor?
What does 'tel' agree with in 'Il court tel un lièvre'?
How do you build a correlative sentence with plus and moins?

activities.title: Comparer et imager
activities.intro: A comparison keeps a link word such as comme, tel or ressembler à. A metaphor removes it and states the image directly, which makes it stronger. Correlative structures with plus and moins let you compare two variables that move together.

items.hr: Son discours était comme une avalanche : impossible à arrêter.
items.en: His speech was like an avalanche: impossible to stop.
items.note: comparaison, marked by comme

items.hr: Cette réforme est un pansement sur une jambe de bois.
items.en: This reform is a plaster on a wooden leg.
items.note: métaphore: no link word

items.hr: Il avance tel un funambule sur un fil.
items.en: He moves forward like a tightrope walker on a wire.
items.note: tel agrees with the noun that follows it

items.hr: La ville se réveille lentement, les paupières lourdes.
items.en: The city wakes up slowly, with heavy eyelids.
items.note: personnification, a metaphor applied to a place

items.hr: Plus le débat avançait, moins les positions se rapprochaient.
items.en: The more the debate went on, the less the positions came closer.
items.note: plus... moins... with no que

activities.title: Choisir la bonne image
activities.intro: Practise distinguishing comparison from metaphor and building correlative structures.

questions.prompt: Which sentence contains a metaphor rather than a comparison?
options: Le marché est un champ de bataille.
Le marché est comme un champ de bataille.
Le marché ressemble à un champ de bataille.
Le marché fait penser à un champ de bataille.
questions.answer: Le marché est un champ de bataille.
questions.explanation: A metaphor states the identity directly, with no comme, ressembler à or other link word.

questions.prompt: In 'Il court tel un lièvre', what does 'tel' agree with?
questions.explanation: Used alone, tel agrees with the noun that comes after it: elle court tel un lièvre, elle court telle une gazelle.

questions.prompt: Complete the correlative structure: ___ on répète un mensonge, plus il paraît vrai.
questions.explanation: Correlative comparisons in French are built with plus... plus... and take no que between the two clauses.

questions.prompt: Insert the one-word link that turns this image into a simile: Il parlait ___ un livre.
questions.explanation: Comme is the basic marker of comparison and keeps the two terms distinct.

questions.prompt: Build the French metaphor for: 'His memory is a sieve.'
questions.explanation: The verb être plus the image with no link word produces a metaphor rather than a comparison.

activities.title: Relire un texte ensemble
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You help a friend sharpen the images in an article she is writing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai écrit : « Le budget de la ville est comme un bateau qui prend l'eau. »
lines.en: I wrote: 'The city budget is like a boat taking in water.'

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: L'image est bonne. Supprime comme : le budget de la ville prend l'eau. C'est plus direct.
lines.en: The image is good. Drop the like: the city budget is taking in water. It is more direct.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour dire que les dépenses augmentent quand les recettes baissent ?
lines.en: And to say that spending rises as revenue falls?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Utilise une corrélation : plus les dépenses augmentent, moins les recettes suivent.
lines.en: Use a correlative: the more spending rises, the less revenue keeps up.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'avais aussi mis : « la mairie avance telle une tortue ».
lines.en: I had also written: 'the town hall moves forward like a tortoise'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Garde-la, telle s'accorde bien avec tortue. Mais une seule image forte par paragraphe.
lines.en: Keep it, telle agrees correctly with tortue. But only one strong image per paragraph.

days.title: Les tournures impersonnelles
days.objective: You can use the impersonal constructions of formal French, choosing correctly between those that take the indicative and those that take the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: Impersonal turns let you state a point without naming who thinks it, which is exactly what reports, minutes and formal emails require.
drills: Rewrite five personal opinions as impersonal statements.
List which impersonal expressions you meet take the subjunctive and which take the indicative.
Open a short written summary with 'il convient de' and close it with 'il importe que'.

items: Why can you never say 'ça s'agit de'?
Which mood follows 'il se peut que' and 'il importe que'?
Give a formal impersonal way to say 'we should remember that'.

activities.title: Le il impersonnel
activities.intro: In these structures il refers to nothing at all: it is a grammatical placeholder. Expressions of necessity, possibility and desirability take the subjunctive, while those presenting a fact as established take the indicative. Il s'agit de has no other possible subject.

items.hr: Il convient de rappeler les faits avant de conclure.
items.en: The facts should be recalled before concluding.
items.note: il convient de + infinitif, formal

items.hr: Il s'agit d'un simple malentendu.
items.en: It is a simple misunderstanding.
items.note: never 'ça s'agit', never a personal subject

items.hr: Il importe que chacun soit informé à temps.
items.en: It is important that everyone be informed in time.
items.note: il importe que + subjonctif

items.hr: Il est apparu que le dossier était incomplet.
items.en: It turned out that the file was incomplete.
items.note: il est apparu que + indicatif, an established fact

items.hr: Il se peut que la livraison prenne du retard.
items.en: The delivery may be delayed.
items.note: il se peut que + subjonctif

activities.title: Formuler sans sujet personnel
activities.intro: Practise building impersonal sentences and choosing the mood each expression requires.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct French?
options: Le texte s'agit d'un problème technique.
questions.explanation: S'agir exists only in the impersonal third person with il; no other subject is possible.

questions.prompt: 'Il importe que chacun ___ ses responsabilités.' Which form is required?
questions.explanation: Il importe que expresses necessity and therefore governs the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Insert the formal impersonal verb meaning 'it is appropriate to': Il ___ de préciser un dernier point.
questions.explanation: Il convient de plus infinitive is the standard formal way to introduce a necessary remark.

questions.prompt: Insert the missing verb of the impersonal expression of possibility: Il se ___ que le vol soit annulé.
questions.explanation: Il se peut que expresses possibility and takes the subjunctive, hence soit annulé.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'It is a matter of principle.'
questions.explanation: S'agir is always followed by de plus a noun phrase, here d'une question de principe.

activities.title: Ouverture d'une réunion
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You chair a formal meeting and use impersonal turns throughout.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nous pouvons commencer ? De quoi parlons-nous exactement aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Can we start? What exactly are we talking about today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il s'agit du budget de fonctionnement pour l'année prochaine.
lines.en: It is about the operating budget for next year.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certains chiffres ont changé depuis la dernière version, je crois.
lines.en: Some figures have changed since the last version, I think.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est apparu que deux postes avaient été comptés deux fois. Il convient donc de reprendre le tableau.
lines.en: It turned out that two items had been counted twice. The table should therefore be reworked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Est-ce que nous devons trancher aujourd'hui ?
lines.en: Do we have to decide today?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il importe que la décision soit prise avant vendredi, mais il se peut que nous ayons besoin d'une seconde réunion.
lines.en: It is important that the decision be taken before Friday, but we may need a second meeting.

days.title: La phrase complexe : la subordination multiple
days.objective: You can build and analyse sentences containing several subordinate clauses, identifying which clause depends on which element.
days.paretoFocus: Written French argument runs on long sentences with nested clauses, and being able to unpack them is what makes reports and articles readable at speed.
drills: Take a long sentence from a French article and sketch its clause tree, arrows from each subordinate to its anchor.
Join four short sentences of your own into one sentence with three subordinate clauses.
Rewrite one overloaded sentence into two clearer ones and compare the effect.

items: How do you tell a relative clause from a completive clause?
Which pronoun refers back to a whole preceding clause?
What tense follows 'quand' when the main verb is in the future?

activities.title: Emboîter les propositions
activities.intro: A complex sentence stacks clauses of different kinds: relative clauses complete a noun, completive clauses complete a verb, and adverbial clauses give the circumstances. Each subordinate clause has its own conjunction and its own verb. Keeping track of which word each clause attaches to is the whole skill.

items.hr: Bien que le rapport soit clair, je crains que certains ne l'aient pas lu, ce qui explique les malentendus.
items.en: Although the report is clear, I fear some people have not read it, which explains the misunderstandings.
items.note: concessive + completive + relative on the whole clause

items.hr: Le responsable, qui savait que la date approchait, a demandé que l'on accélère.
items.en: The manager, who knew the deadline was coming, asked us to speed up.
items.note: relative containing its own completive

items.hr: Comme il pleuvait quand nous sommes partis, nous avons pris le train qui passait à midi.
items.en: Since it was raining when we left, we took the train that ran at noon.
items.note: cause + time + relative

items.hr: Ce que je propose, c'est que nous attendions le rapport que le comité prépare.
items.en: What I propose is that we wait for the report the committee is preparing.
items.note: ce que in front position, then completive, then relative

items.hr: Il affirme qu'il ignorait que le contrat avait été signé.
items.en: He claims he did not know that the contract had been signed.
items.note: one completive inside another

activities.title: Analyser et construire
activities.intro: Practise identifying each subordinate clause and assembling long sentences correctly.

questions.prompt: In 'Je pense que l'homme qui a téléphoné voulait un rendez-vous', what is 'qui a téléphoné'?
questions.explanation: Qui introduces a relative clause attached to the noun l'homme, inside the completive introduced by que.

questions.prompt: Which sentence contains three subordinate clauses?
options: Il dit qu'il viendra quand il aura fini le travail que tu lui as confié.
Il dit qu'il viendra demain matin.
Il viendra quand il aura fini.
Il a terminé le travail hier soir.
questions.answer: Il dit qu'il viendra quand il aura fini le travail que tu lui as confié.
questions.explanation: It contains a completive with qu'il viendra, a time clause with quand, and a relative with que tu lui as confié.

questions.prompt: Insert the relative pronoun that refers back to the entire previous clause: Il a refusé de signer, ___ a surpris tout le monde.
questions.explanation: Ce qui takes up a whole preceding clause as its antecedent, unlike qui which needs a noun.

questions.prompt: Insert the time conjunction that fits a futur antérieur in the subordinate clause: Nous partirons ___ nous aurons reçu la confirmation.
questions.explanation: French uses a real future or futur antérieur after these time conjunctions, unlike English which uses a present.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I believe that the person who called did not leave a message.'
questions.explanation: The relative clause qui a appelé sits inside the completive introduced by que, right after its antecedent.

activities.title: Point sur un dossier difficile
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You brief a colleague on a delayed file, answering in long, clearly built sentences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Où en est le dossier que la direction attend depuis mars ?
lines.en: Where do things stand on the file management has been waiting for since March?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il avance, mais je crains que la partie juridique, qui dépend d'un avis extérieur, ne soit pas prête.
lines.en: It is progressing, but I fear the legal part, which depends on an outside opinion, will not be ready.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et si cet avis arrive en retard ?
lines.en: And if that opinion arrives late?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Comme nous ne pouvons rien décider tant qu'il manque, nous devrons reporter la réunion que tu as fixée.
lines.en: Since we cannot decide anything while it is missing, we will have to postpone the meeting you scheduled.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: La direction risque de mal le prendre.
lines.en: Management may take it badly.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'expliquerai que le retard vient d'un cabinet extérieur, ce qui devrait suffire à les rassurer.
lines.en: I will explain that the delay comes from an outside firm, which should be enough to reassure them.

days.title: La reprise anaphorique : éviter les répétitions
days.objective: You can refer back to something already mentioned using demonstratives, summarising nouns and expressions like ce dernier, instead of repeating the same word.
days.paretoFocus: French written style penalises repetition heavily, so mastering anaphora is what makes your paragraphs read as native rather than as a list of restarts.
drills: Take a paragraph you wrote and replace every repeated noun with a different reference.
Collect five summarising nouns such as ce texte, cette décision, ce phénomène.
Read a news paragraph and mark every expression that refers back to the first sentence.

items: Who does 'ce dernier' refer to when two people have just been mentioned?
How does a summarising noun differ from a simple pronoun?
Give two ways to refer back to the first and second items of a pair.

activities.title: Reprendre sans répéter
activities.intro: French avoids repeating a noun by picking it up in a new form. Ce dernier points to the most recently mentioned element. A summarising noun such as ce texte or cette décision both refers back and adds an interpretation. Ordinals like la première and la seconde split a pair.

items.hr: Le ministre a présenté son plan. Ce dernier prévoit trois mesures.
items.en: The minister presented his plan. The latter provides for three measures.
items.note: ce dernier = the most recently mentioned element

items.hr: Une nouvelle loi a été votée hier. Ce texte entrera en vigueur en janvier.
items.en: A new law was passed yesterday. This text will come into force in January.
items.note: a generic noun replaces the repeated one

items.hr: Il a démissionné sans prévenir. Cette décision a surpris ses proches.
items.en: He resigned without warning. This decision surprised those close to him.
items.note: summarising noun that also interprets

items.hr: Les habitants ont protesté ; certains ont même saisi la justice.
items.en: The residents protested; some even took legal action.
items.note: certains picks up part of the group

items.hr: L'entreprise a ouvert deux usines : la première en Bretagne, la seconde en Alsace.
items.en: The company opened two factories: the first in Brittany, the second in Alsace.
items.note: ordinals to split a pair without repeating usine

activities.title: Alléger un paragraphe
activities.intro: Pick the substitute that best replaces each repeated word below.

questions.prompt: In 'Marie a rencontré Julie ; cette dernière était en retard', who was late?
options: on ne peut pas le savoir
questions.explanation: Ce dernier and cette dernière always point to the element mentioned most recently, here Julie.

questions.prompt: Which expression best replaces the repetition in 'Le gouvernement a publié un décret. Le décret entre en vigueur lundi.'?
questions.explanation: A generic noun of the same category, introduced by a demonstrative, is the standard way to avoid repeating décret.

questions.prompt: Insert the demonstrative adjective that introduces the summarising noun: Il a refusé toute aide. ___ décision a étonné ses proches.
questions.explanation: The demonstrative marks that the noun sums up what has just been said rather than introducing something new.

questions.prompt: Refer back to the second of the two people just mentioned: Paul a écrit à Luc ; ce ___ n'a jamais répondu.
questions.explanation: Ce dernier designates the last element named, so here it is Luc who never replied.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'The company hired two engineers; the first one starts on Monday.'
questions.explanation: The ordinal la première refers back to one of the two engineers without repeating the noun.

activities.title: Relecture d'un article
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An editor points out repetitions in a draft and you propose anaphoric replacements.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu écris quatre fois « le maire » dans le même paragraphe.
lines.en: You write 'the mayor' four times in the same paragraph.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je remplace la deuxième occurrence par « l'élu » et la troisième par « ce dernier ».
lines.en: I will replace the second occurrence with 'the elected official' and the third with 'the latter'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et la phrase où tu répètes « la fermeture de la piscine » ?
lines.en: And the sentence where you repeat 'the closure of the swimming pool'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'écrirai : cette mesure a provoqué une pétition. Le nom résume et interprète.
lines.en: I will write: this measure triggered a petition. The noun sums up and interprets.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il reste les deux associations que tu cites l'une après l'autre.
lines.en: There are still the two associations you quote one after the other.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: La première réclame un moratoire, la seconde demande un référendum. Plus aucune répétition.
lines.en: The first calls for a moratorium, the second asks for a referendum. No repetition left.

days.title: La ponctuation et le rythme de la phrase
days.objective: You can use French punctuation deliberately, controlling the rhythm of a sentence with commas, colons and semicolons, and name each mark in French.
days.paretoFocus: Punctuation is the last visible difference between a competent text and a native one, and misplaced commas change meaning as surely as a wrong word does.
drills: Take one long sentence and repunctuate it three ways, then read each aloud.
Find an appositive phrase in a French text and check the commas that isolate it.
Write a short paragraph in which one sentence is very short, for effect.

items: Which mark announces an explanation or a list?
What do commas around a phrase such as 'tous très jeunes' signal?
What is the French name for the mark written as a semicolon?

activities.title: Rythmer une phrase
activities.intro: Punctuation in French is not decoration: it directs the reader's breathing and marks grammatical structure. Commas isolate added elements, the colon announces what follows, and the semicolon links two clauses that are complete but closely tied. Short sentences after long ones create emphasis.

items.hr: Il hésita, longuement, puis signa.
items.en: He hesitated, at length, then signed.
items.note: commas slow the rhythm and isolate the adverb

items.hr: Une seule chose comptait : partir.
items.en: Only one thing mattered: leaving.
items.note: the colon announces the answer

items.hr: Le résultat ? Personne ne l'attendait.
items.en: The result? Nobody expected it.
items.note: a short question creates a break in rhythm

items.hr: Il avait tout prévu ; il avait pourtant tout raté.
items.en: He had planned everything; and yet he had failed at everything.
items.note: the semicolon links two balanced clauses

items.hr: Les trois candidats, tous très jeunes, ont refusé le poste.
items.en: The three candidates, all very young, turned down the post.
items.note: paired commas isolate an apposition

activities.title: Ponctuer avec intention
activities.intro: Practise naming French punctuation marks and choosing the one the sentence needs.

questions.prompt: Which punctuation mark announces an explanation or a list?
questions.explanation: The colon opens onto what follows, whether an explanation, a list or a quotation.

questions.prompt: In 'Les élèves, fatigués, sont partis tôt', what do the commas do?
questions.explanation: The paired commas mark fatigués as an added descriptive element that could be removed.

questions.prompt: Complete the French name of the mark written as ';' : le point-___
questions.explanation: The semicolon is called point-virgule and joins two closely related independent clauses.

questions.prompt: Complete with the name of the marks that enclose a quotation in French: les mots cités sont placés entre ___.
questions.explanation: French quotation marks are called guillemets and are usually preceded and followed by a space.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Only one thing mattered: leaving.'
questions.explanation: The colon announces the infinitive that answers the expectation created by the first clause.

activities.title: Corriger une lettre
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend asks you to check the punctuation of an important letter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ma première phrase fait six lignes. Tu la trouves lisible ?
lines.en: My first sentence is six lines long. Do you find it readable?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Coupe-la en deux et garde une phrase courte à la fin. Le lecteur respire.
lines.en: Split it in two and keep a short sentence at the end. The reader can breathe.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ici, j'annonce trois arguments. Je mets une virgule ?
lines.en: And here, I announce three arguments. Should I put a comma?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, des deux-points. Ils annoncent la liste qui suit.
lines.en: No, a colon. It announces the list that follows.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'ai aussi écrit : « les candidats tous diplômés ont postulé ».
lines.en: I also wrote: 'the candidates all qualified applied'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il faut deux virgules autour de « tous diplômés ». Sans elles, la phrase change de sens.
lines.en: You need two commas around 'all qualified'. Without them, the sentence changes meaning.

days.title: L'intelligence artificielle et la société
days.objective: Discuss the social impact of artificial intelligence while conceding a point before pushing back, using avoir beau, certes ... mais, quand bien même and loin de.
days.paretoFocus: Conceding first and objecting second is the single most persuasive move in French debate; these four formulas cover almost every written and spoken concession.
drills: Write three sentences about a technology you use, each starting with 'X a beau ...' followed by a real drawback.
Take one opinion you disagree with and answer it in the pattern 'Certes, ..., mais ...'.
Say out loud two predictions about automation using 'quand bien même' plus a conditional verb.

items: Which concession structure is followed by an infinitive rather than a clause?
What mood follows 'quand bien même'?
How do you say 'the fact remains that' in formal French?

activities.title: Concéder avant de contester
activities.intro: In French argument, you rarely attack an idea head on. You first grant part of it, then reverse the perspective. These five patterns all do that, but they attach to different structures: an infinitive, a clause, a conditional, or a noun.

items.hr: L'intelligence artificielle a beau progresser vite, elle reste incapable de juger un contexte.
items.en: Artificial intelligence may well be progressing fast, but it is still incapable of judging a context.
items.note: avoir beau + infinitif, no 'bien que' and no subjunctive needed

items.hr: Certes, ces outils font gagner du temps, mais ils déresponsabilisent ceux qui les utilisent.
items.en: Admittedly these tools save time, but they take responsibility away from the people who use them.
items.note: certes ... mais is the standard written concession pair

items.hr: Quand bien même l'algorithme serait neutre, les données qu'on lui fournit ne le sont pas.
items.en: Even supposing the algorithm were neutral, the data we feed it is not.
items.note: quand bien même + conditionnel, stronger than 'même si'

items.hr: Il n'en reste pas moins que la décision finale doit revenir à un humain.
items.en: The fact remains that the final decision must lie with a human being.
items.note: il n'en reste pas moins que + indicatif, closes a concession

items.hr: Loin de supprimer le travail, l'automatisation en déplace la nature.
items.en: Far from eliminating work, automation shifts its nature.
items.note: loin de + infinitif rejects an assumption before stating your own

activities.title: Nuancer un débat technologique
activities.intro: Practise the four concession structures in sentences about automation and algorithms.

questions.prompt: Choisissez la forme correcte : « L'algorithme ___ être performant, il reproduit nos préjugés. »
questions.explanation: 'Avoir beau' plus an infinitive expresses concession: however well the algorithm performs, the objection still holds.

questions.prompt: Quel mode suit « quand bien même » dans : « Quand bien même la machine ___ plus vite, elle ne comprendrait rien » ?
questions.explanation: 'Quand bien même' takes the conditional, not the subjunctive, and the main clause is also conditional.

questions.prompt: Complétez avec cinq mots : « Ces systèmes sont utiles ; il ___ que leur opacité pose un vrai problème. »
questions.explanation: 'Il n'en reste pas moins que' plus the indicative concedes the previous point while keeping your objection intact.

questions.prompt: Complétez avec deux mots : « ___ résoudre le problème du travail, cette technologie le déplace. »
questions.explanation: 'Loin de' plus an infinitive denies the expected effect before you give the real one.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'Admittedly the tool is fast, but it explains nothing.'
questions.explanation: 'Certes' opens the concession and 'mais' introduces the objection; the negation is 'ne ... rien' around the verb.

activities.title: Faut-il confier nos décisions aux machines ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner is enthusiastic about automated decision making; concede a point each time before objecting.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Franchement, ces systèmes décident mieux que nous, et beaucoup plus vite.
lines.en: Honestly, these systems decide better than we do, and much faster.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ils ont beau décider vite, personne ne peut expliquer comment ils arrivent à leurs conclusions.
lines.en: They may well decide fast, but nobody can explain how they reach their conclusions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On finira bien par comprendre leur fonctionnement, c'est une question de temps.
lines.en: We will end up understanding how they work, it is only a matter of time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quand bien même on le comprendrait, il faudrait encore savoir qui assume les erreurs.
lines.en: Even supposing we understood it, we would still need to know who takes responsibility for the mistakes.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certes, la responsabilité reste floue, mais les humains se trompent bien davantage.
lines.en: Admittedly responsibility remains unclear, but humans make far more mistakes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Loin de nier ça, je dis simplement que nos erreurs, elles, sont contestables devant un juge.
lines.en: Far from denying that, I am simply saying that our mistakes can at least be challenged before a judge.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu es donc contre leur utilisation dans l'administration ?
lines.en: So you are against using them in public administration?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pas du tout ; il n'en reste pas moins qu'un dossier doit pouvoir être revu par une personne.
lines.en: Not at all; the fact remains that a case must be reviewable by a person.

days.title: La bioéthique et les limites de la science
days.objective: Express doubt, reservation and conditional acceptance about medical research using the subjunctive after negated opinions, peu probable, sans que and encore faut-il que.
days.paretoFocus: Bioethical debate is built on hedged claims; knowing exactly which triggers flip a verb into the subjunctive is what makes cautious French sound educated rather than vague.
drills: Turn three confident statements into doubtful ones with 'Je ne suis pas convaincu que ...'.
Write two sentences with 'sans que' describing something that happened without public debate.
Say three things you consider unlikely, each starting with 'Il est peu probable que ...'.

items: Why does 'il est probable que' take the indicative while 'il est peu probable que' takes the subjunctive?
Which mood follows 'sans que'?
How do you add a necessary condition after granting a point?

activities.title: Exprimer le doute et la réserve
activities.intro: French marks uncertainty grammatically, not just lexically. Once probability drops or an opinion verb is negated, the following verb goes into the subjunctive. Certainty keeps the indicative, and the contrast between the two is meaningful.

items.hr: Je ne suis pas convaincu que la loi puisse suivre le rythme des laboratoires.
items.en: I am not convinced that the law can keep up with the pace of the laboratories.
items.note: negated opinion verb triggers the subjunctive: pouvoir devient puisse

items.hr: Il est peu probable qu'on parvienne un jour à un consensus mondial sur ces questions.
items.en: It is unlikely that we will ever reach a global consensus on these questions.
items.note: peu probable + subjonctif, but probable + indicatif

items.hr: On modifie le vivant sans que la société ait vraiment tranché la question de principe.
items.en: We are modifying living organisms without society having really settled the question of principle.
items.note: sans que + subjonctif, here the subjunctive past

items.hr: Que la recherche avance, personne ne le conteste ; encore faut-il qu'elle soit encadrée.
items.en: Nobody disputes that research is moving forward; it still has to be properly regulated.
items.note: encore faut-il que + subjonctif adds a condition after a concession

items.hr: Je doute fort que le simple fait d'être possible rende une pratique légitime.
items.en: I very much doubt that merely being possible makes a practice legitimate.
items.note: douter que + subjonctif, rendre devient rende

activities.title: Le subjonctif de l'incertitude
activities.intro: Practise choosing between indicative and subjunctive in sentences about medical ethics.

questions.prompt: Complétez : « Il est peu probable que le comité ___ un avis avant l'été. »
questions.explanation: Low probability triggers the subjunctive, so 'rendre' becomes 'rende'.

questions.prompt: Laquelle de ces phrases emploie correctement l'indicatif ?
options: Il est probable qu'on aboutira à un compromis.
Il est peu probable qu'on aboutira à un compromis.
Je ne pense pas qu'on aboutira à un compromis.
Bien qu'on aboutira à un compromis.
questions.answer: Il est probable qu'on aboutira à un compromis.
questions.explanation: Only positive 'il est probable que' keeps the indicative; low probability, negated opinion and 'bien que' all require the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complétez avec deux mots : « On a autorisé cette technique ___ le moindre débat public ait eu lieu. »
questions.explanation: 'Sans que' introduces an absent circumstance and always takes the subjunctive, here 'ait eu lieu'.

questions.prompt: Mettez le verbe « pouvoir » à la forme qui convient : « Je ne crois pas qu'un seul comité ___ trancher une question pareille. »
questions.explanation: 'Ne pas croire que' negates an opinion, so the following verb is subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the French for: 'I doubt that the law is enough.'
questions.explanation: 'Douter que' takes the subjunctive: 'suffire' becomes 'suffise'.

activities.title: Jusqu'où peut aller la recherche ?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner defends unrestricted research; answer with hedged doubt using subjunctive triggers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Si une technique existe et qu'elle soigne, il faut l'autoriser, c'est évident.
lines.en: If a technique exists and it cures people, it should be allowed, that is obvious.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je doute que l'existence d'une technique suffise à en justifier l'usage.
